disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Stridor only with agitation and ABSENT at rest; no significant work of breathing at rest; alert, consolable, normal colour — mild croup, not severe airway obstruction
trigger_question_examples: Is the noisy breathing there only when he's upset/crying, or also when he's calm and resting?
At rest, is he breathing comfortably with his ribs not sucking in and normal lip colour?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The noisy breathing is only when he cries — when he's calm and resting he breathes comfortably, ribs not sucking in, lips normal colour.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No drooling, able to swallow saliva, no muffled 'hot-potato' voice (barking cough present), no tripod/'sniffing' posture, not toxic — argues against epiglottitis
trigger_question_examples: Is he drooling or unable to swallow his spit, or sitting forward to breathe?
Does he have a barking cough (croup) or is his voice muffled and is he very unwell/toxic?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No drooling, he swallows his spit fine, he's got the barking cough, and he's not sitting forward or very toxic.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Preceding coryzal viral prodrome over 2 days, gradual onset — not a sudden unheralded choking episode (argues against foreign body)
trigger_question_examples: Did this build up after a cold over a couple of days, or start suddenly with a choking episode while eating/playing??
Can you tell me a bit more about that?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on after a cold over a couple of days — no sudden choking episode.
